_note: 2026-07-22 batch (batch 5 of an ongoing 100+-program research pass), following user direction to try a genuinely different angle: non-English-speaking-country branches of Zionist youth movements. This was the highest-yield angle since batch 1 -- the DB's existing World Bnei Akiva and Habonim Dror coverage (Kadima/Limmud/Torani/MTA/Mechina Olamit; UK/Australia/Southern Africa/North America Shnat) turned out to be entirely Anglophone-country-facing, with the Latin American and French/Spanish-speaking tracks of both movements completely unrepresented. A French Bnei Akiva program ('Shalhevet/Ahchara', mentioned on worldbneiakiva.org/france-fr) and a Brazil-specific Habonim Dror domain (habonimdrorbrasil.com) were researched but rejected -- both had DNS resolution failures (domain does not resolve at all), not just bot-blocking, so no live site could be confirmed for either. masaisrael.org pages return HTTP 403 to a bare curl request (Cloudflare bot-blocking, same as several masaisrael.org pages in batch 2) but were independently confirmed live via WebFetch, which retrieved specific, current content including named 2026-2027 and 2027-2028 cohort dates -- not stale/cached text.

youth_movements.name: Oz (World Bnei Akiva)
youth_movements.location: Israel (study at Yeshivat Hakotel and Midreshet Lindenbaum, Jerusalem)
youth_movements.description: A 10-month religious-Zionist gap year hachshara program for Bnei Akiva members from Latin America (Chile, Argentina, Uruguay, Brazil), combining Torah study, Zionist education, and leadership development with seminars and excursions across Israel.
youth_movements.cost: ~$6,000 USD (excludes airfare and some medical costs); Masa grants available
youth_movements.signupInstructions: Apply via the World Bnei Akiva website, then register with Masa; a personal interview follows.
youth_movements.adminNote: Confirmed genuinely new -- the DB's existing World Bnei Akiva entries (Kadima, Limmud, Torani, MTA, Mechina Olamit, Bnei Akiva Olamit: Shalem) are all Anglophone-country programs; Oz is specifically the Latin American track and was not previously represented.

youth_movements.name: Amijai (World Bnei Akiva)
youth_movements.description: An 8-month religious-Zionist gap year hachshara program for Bnei Akiva members from Spanish-speaking countries (primarily Mexico and Panama), building leadership skills and connection to Israel, Judaism, and Zionism.
youth_movements.cost: ~$14,000 USD after Masa grant; excludes airfare and some medical costs
youth_movements.signupInstructions: Apply online, then register with Masa; a personal interview follows.
youth_movements.adminNote: Confirmed genuinely new, same rationale as Oz above -- the Spanish-speaking-Mexico/Panama track, distinct from every existing World Bnei Akiva entry.

youth_movements.name: Habonim Dror Latin America - Shnat
youth_movements.description: A leadership-training gap year (shnat hachshara) for Habonim Dror members from Brazil, Argentina, and Uruguay, combining practical experience, Zionist-socialist education, and movement leadership preparation.
youth_movements.signupInstructions: Apply via the program's Masa Israel page; cohorts run August-to-July.
youth_movements.adminNote: Confirmed genuinely new -- the DB's existing Habonim Dror entries (UK, Australia, Southern Africa, North America 'Workshop') are all Anglophone-country programs; this is the Latin American track, run as a joint Brazil/Argentina/Uruguay cohort rather than one country at a time (476 alumni cited on the program page). A dedicated Habonim Dror Brasil domain (habonimdrorbrasil.com) was found via search but does not resolve at all (DNS failure) -- this masaisrael.org page was used instead as the only independently-confirmed-live source, and lists active 2026-2027 and 2027-2028 cohort dates.
